name: Flaky Pie Dough
recipeInstructions:
To make the dough:
1. Place the diced butter and shortening in the freezer while you prepare the flour and water.
2. Fill a 2-cup measuring cup with ice cubes and water for the water measurement. Set aside.
3. In a food processor bowl, mix together the flour, salt, and sugar.
4. Add the cut-up butter and shortening in pieces. Pulse the machine until the mixture is cornmeal textured with pea-sized pieces of butter.
5. Measure out the ice water into two separate amounts.
6. Pour about half the measured ice water into a large mixing bowl containing the flour mixture. Sprinkle the remaining water over the top.
7. Using both hands, toss the dough to ensure even moisture. Squeeze a handful of dough; if it clumps together, it is moist enough. If not, add more water and toss again.
8. Turn the dough onto a clean table, scraping the bowl well. Gently mash the dough with your heel using a few quick strokes.
9. Gather the dough into a rough log shape. Score it into three equal pieces and cut each piece so that one is about 1/3 (9 oz.) and the other two are about 2/3 (18 oz.).
10. Form the smaller piece into a patty about 4 to 5 inches in diameter. Roll out the larger piece until it reaches a diameter of 12 inches.
11. Place the smaller disc on a floured surface, roll it to a 12-inch diameter, and gently mold it around the sides without stretching. Trim any excess dough so that it is flush with the edge of the pie plate.
12. Freeze the formed crust for at least four hours or overnight.
To make the top crust:
1. Refrigerate one half of the log of dough.
2. On a floured surface, roll out the remaining dough to a thickness of about 3/16 inch (less than 1/4 inch).
3. Using a 3-inch heart cutter, cut as many shapes as possible without stretching the dough. Layer the cut-outs on parchment or wax paper-lined sheets.
4. Roll out the second piece and repeat the cutting process.
5. You will need approximately 24 large hearts, 5 medium, and 4 small pieces. Freeze the top crust until solid.
Store both the bottom and top crusts in the freezer for up to one week before using.
